fault_hypothesis: The `overlaps` function on line 64 uses strict inequalities (`b[1] <= a[0]` and `b[0] >= a[1]`), which treats adjacent windows (where end equals start) as non-overlapping. The failing tests expect adjacent boundaries to be treated as overlapping, so the condition should use strict less-than (`b[1] < a[0]` and `b[0] > a[1]`).

constraints.intent: Check that overlaps returns True when candidate start equals existing end (adjacent boundary), which the buggy strict inequality makes False.
After the non-overlap branch, ensure no adjacent-boundary case was incorrectly passed through as non-overlapping.
At each loop iteration tail, verify no adjacent-boundary slot was processed without triggering the overlap return.

spec.expr: all(not (merged__1[i][1] == merged__1[j][0] or merged__1[j][1] == merged__1[i][0]) for i in range(len(merged__1)) for j in range(i+1, len(merged__1)))

constraints.intent: After building merged, confirm no adjacent-boundary windows exist, which would indicate a candidate that should have been rejected was accepted.
